page: Microsoft Azure HC44-32rs is an HCS family server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8168 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at 2.7 GHz, with a thread-per-core ratio of 1.0. The instance is equipped with 352.0 GB of system memory, providing 11.0 GB of RAM per core. It includes 717 GB of local storage, supports Premium Storage, and is RDMA capable, running under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

Benchmark data shows strong performance in memory bandwidth for large blocks, both for read-only (208.35 GB/sec) and write (86.77 GB/sec) operations, placing it in the top 25%. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark database operations also rank in the top 25%. However, single-threaded performance is more moderate, with gzip compression scoring in the bottom 25%. Large language model text generation speeds are poor, falling into the bottom 10%, though prompt processing speeds remain in the middle 50% average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the HC44-32rs offers a high memory-to-core ratio of 11.0 GB per core and dedicated physical compute resources, making it suitable for memory-intensive workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading. The inclusion of RDMA capability and local storage provides high-throughput networking and data access. This server is suited for database operations, multi-threaded data decompression, and memory-bound scientific or engineering simulations, while it is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or active LLM text generation.
description: Microsoft Azure HC44-32rs is an HCS family server featuring an Intel Xeon 8168 processor with 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs running at 2.7 GHz. It is configured with 352.0 GB of memory, providing 11.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside 717 GB of local storage. This x86_64 architecture instance supports RDMA and Premium Storage under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmarks indicate strong performance in large-block memory bandwidth and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, both placing in the top 25%. However, single-threaded gzip compression and LLM text generation speeds represent notable performance tradeoffs, scoring in the lower tiers. With its dedicated CPU allocation and high memory density, this server is suited for database operations, parallel processing, and memory-bound engineering workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure HC44-32rs is an HCS family server with 32 dedicated Intel Xeon 8168 cores, 352 GB RAM, and 717 GB local storage, optimized for database operations and high memory bandwidth workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure HC44-32rs features 32 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 352 GB RAM, and RDMA capability, delivering strong memory bandwidth for databases.
tagline: A dedicated 32-core x86_64 server featuring high memory capacity, RDMA capability, and strong large-block memory bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.

352.0 GB of system memory providing 11.0 GB of RAM per core
717 GB of local storage with Premium Storage and RDMA capabilities
